extra.message: User-controlled input (student name from POST request) is interpolated into SQL query using Python string formatting (% operator). The value of 'name' is substituted into the query string before it is passed to cursor.execute(), allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary SQL. For example, a name value of `'); DROP TABLE students; --` would execute dropped table. Compare with the parameterized queries used elsewhere (e.g., Student.get, Review.create).

Jinja2 template engine is configured with autoescape=False, disabling automatic HTML escaping for all templates. This allows user-controlled data rendered in templates without explicit '| e' filters to be interpreted as HTML/JavaScript, enabling Cross-Site Scripting attacks. Multiple templates render user-controlled data (student names, course titles, descriptions, review text, user names) without explicit escaping.

Course title (user-controlled via Course.create from POST data) is rendered without HTML escaping. With autoescape=False in the Jinja2 configuration, this allows XSS if a course title contains malicious HTML/JavaScript.
Course description (user-controlled via Course.create from POST data) is rendered without HTML escaping. With autoescape=False, this allows XSS.

Review text (user-controlled via Review.create from POST form data) is rendered without HTML escaping. With autoescape=False, an attacker can submit a review containing <script> tags that will execute in other users' browsers.
Course title (user-controlled) rendered without escaping on the course detail page, enabling XSS.
Course description (user-controlled) rendered without escaping on the course detail page, enabling XSS.
Student name (user-controlled via Student.create from POST data) is rendered without HTML escaping. Combined with autoescape=False, this allows stored XSS through student names.

User first_name, middle_name, and last_name are rendered without HTML escaping in the base template navigation bar. If user names in the database contain malicious content, it will execute as HTML/JavaScript in every page for authenticated users.
Course title rendered without escaping in the review form template, enabling XSS.

The csrf_middleware is commented out in the application middleware stack. A CSRF middleware implementation exists in sqli/middlewares.py but is not enabled. All POST endpoints (login, student creation, course creation, review creation, evaluation, logout) are vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks.

The POST handler for /students/ creates a new student without any authentication or authorization check. While the template conditionally shows the form to authenticated users, the server-side handler does not use @authorize() or check get_auth_user(). Any unauthenticated user can create students by sending a POST request.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The POST handler for /courses/ creates a new course without any authentication or authorization check. The template shows this form only to admin users (auth_user.is_admin), but the server-side handler does not enforce this. Any unauthenticated user can create courses by sending a POST request, bypassing the intended admin-only restriction.
The POST handler for /courses/{course_id}/review creates a review without any authentication check. Any unauthenticated user can submit reviews for any course.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The POST handler for /students/{student_id}/evaluate/{course_id} creates marks (grades) without any authentication or authorization check. The template shows this form only to admin users, but the server does not enforce this. Any unauthenticated user can assign grades to any student for any course.

Password verification uses unsalted MD5 hashing (md5(password.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()). MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password hashing. No salt is used, making the hashes vulnerable to rainbow table and brute-force attacks. Should use a dedicated password hashing function like bcrypt, scrypt, or argon2.

cwe: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

extra.message: Database credentials (username 'postgres' and password 'postgres') are hardcoded in the configuration file. These are real database credentials that could grant full access to the PostgreSQL database if leaked.
The aiohttp Application is created with debug=True, which enables detailed error pages and debug information that can expose internal application details to attackers.

The Redis session storage is initialized with httponly=False, which means the session cookie is accessible to JavaScript via document.cookie. If an XSS vulnerability is exploited, the attacker can steal the session cookie and hijack the user's session.

The 50x error template renders the full exception __dict__ attribute: {{ error.__dict__ }}. This can expose sensitive internal information including stack traces, file paths, database queries, and configuration details. Combined with autoescape=False, this also enables XSS through error attributes.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
